methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Fervo Energy is a pre-IPO climate and energy company founded by Tim Latimer and Jack Norbeck that has raised $1.5B+ to date. The target operates within a sector containing 21 publicly traded and private peers, though the comparable universe for direct valuation is constrained: only 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers identified, with top-tier peers including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and QuantumScape.

Peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting screening criteria. The narrow peer set—requiring either exact stage alignment, similar capitalization tier, or both—yielded fewer than three datapoints necessary for median-based analysis. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive heterogeneity across different technology maturity levels and business models within climate and energy, rendering results unreliable for valuation purposes.

The analysis framework does not support a point estimate or range at this stage. Resolution requires either: (i) expansion of private comparable data within the pre-IPO frontier-tech cohort; (ii) application of discounted cash flow or sum-of-the-parts methods contingent on business model specificity; or (iii) forward-looking precedent transaction analysis if M&A or IPO comparables emerge. The key uncertainty remains the scarcity of direct peer comparables, which limits triangulation to qualitative peer positioning rather than quantitative benchmarking.
